inputs.name: req_pool_indices
inputs.meaning: Indices for the required token pool used for accessing embeddings or representations of tokens.
inputs.inference_role: Provides the indices to the token pool for each sequence in the batch.

inputs.name: req_to_token
inputs.meaning: Mapping of request indices to token representations in the token pool.
inputs.inference_role: Facilitates lookup of token representations for each request.

inputs.name: paged_kernel_lens
inputs.meaning: Lengths of sequences in a paged format, defining how many tokens each sequence contains.
inputs.inference_role: Used to determine how many tokens to process for each sequence.

inputs.name: kv_indices
inputs.meaning: Indices for the key-value pairs that are being generated and populated in this kernel.
inputs.shape: int32 tensor of shape [speculative_num_steps, num_seqs, topk]
inputs.inference_role: Stores the indices pointing to the generated key-value representations.

inputs.name: kv_indptr
inputs.meaning: Pointer indices for accessing the key-value pairs, determining how many keys belong to each request.
inputs.inference_role: Maintains the offsets for accessing the key-value pairs for each sequence.

inputs.name: positions
inputs.meaning: Tracks the positions of tokens in the sequence, often used for attention mechanisms.
inputs.inference_role: Facilitates position encoding to ensure tokens are aware of their relative positions.

inputs.name: pool_len
inputs.meaning: Length of the token pool, defines the range of valid indices in the token pool.
inputs.inference_role: Sets the upper limit for indexing in the token pool.

inputs.name: kv_indices_stride
inputs.meaning: Stride in the kv_indices tensor, indicating step size for traversing the second dimension.
inputs.inference_role: Used to determine the appropriate offset when storing multiple sequences' indices.

inputs.name: kv_indptr_stride
inputs.meaning: Stride in the kv_indptr tensor, indicating step size for traversing the key-value pointer structure.
inputs.inference_role: Helps in indexing multiple sequences for the key-value pointers.

inputs.name: bs_upper
inputs.meaning: Upper limit for the batch size, typically a power of two for optimization.
inputs.inference_role: Defines the range of sequences to process in a single kernel call.

inputs.name: iter_upper
inputs.meaning: Upper limit for the number of iterations in speculative decoding, typically a power of two.
inputs.inference_role: Specifies the maximum number of iterations to run in the kernel.

inputs.name: num_tokens_upper
inputs.meaning: Upper limit for the total number of tokens processed, also optimized to be a power of two.
inputs.inference_role: Sets bounds for efficient token processing in memory.

inputs.name: page_size
inputs.meaning: Defines the size of pages for accessing token batches, impacting memory access patterns.
inputs.inference_role: Optimizes loading and storing data to/from the token pool.

outputs.name: kv_indices
outputs.meaning: Populated tensor representing the indices of key-value pairs generated during decoding.
outputs.shape: int32 tensor of shape [speculative_num_steps, num_seqs, topk]
outputs.inference_role: Used to access the generated key-value representations during model inference.

outputs.name: kv_indptr
outputs.meaning: Updated pointer indices for accessing the corresponding key-value pairs for each request.
outputs.inference_role: Used for determining where to access key-value pairs in the main memory.
